# UC-13.4.8 · Microsoft 365 Copilot Data Exfiltration Risk (ESCU)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the workplace assistant that reads and summarizes your company documents. When someone who can open many locations suddenly pulls together a large pile of restricted material in a short time, we raise a clear signal so reviewers can step in before sensitive content spreads in ways policy did not intend.*

---

## Description

Detects Microsoft 365 Copilot data exfiltration risk by correlating unified audit Workload Copilot events—including CopilotInteraction, Search, FileDownload, and FileSyncDownloadedFull—with Microsoft Purview sensitivity labels, DLP policy matches on sourcetype o365 purview dlp, Defender for Cloud Apps risky activities on o365 mcas, Enterprise Security ESCU Microsoft Cloud notables via Alerts summaries, and Entra ID sign-in context. Copilot grounding on SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams, email, and Loop can surface Highly Confidential or Restricted documents through summarization or generation; users with wide read access become indirect exfil channels even without malware. The analytic distinguishes prompt-driven model abuse (sibling UC-13.4.9) from insider misuse of legitimate Copilot features, and distinguishes API latency (UC-13.4.1), token cost (UC-13.4.2), GPU utilization (UC-13.4.3), model version tracking (UC-13.4.4), gateway throttles (UC-13.4.5), Ollama abuse (UC-13.4.6), MCP suspicious tool calls (UC-13.4.7), key rotation (UC-13.4.10), content policy violations (UC-13.4.11), seq2seq anomalies (UC-13.4.13), and MLTK drift (UC-13.4.15). Eyes-on eDiscovery review and Copilot interaction lifecycle semantics are first-class inputs.

## Value

Security, compliance, and insider-threat programs gain a tamper-resistant ledger that ties Copilot retrieval and download telemetry to Purview labels, MCAS scores, ESCU Microsoft Cloud stories, and Entra risk before sensitive narratives leave the tenant boundary. Executives receive measurable assurance that Highly Confidential grounding is monitored with executive-specific severity, while legal and HR retain defensible workflows for supervised review windows. Platform teams reduce mean time to containment by fusing burst analytics with inventory-grounded baselines instead of isolated audit screens.

## Implementation

Ingest o365 audit Copilot workload, o365 purview dlp, o365 mcas, azure eventhub signin, and accelerate Alerts for ESCU fusion; maintain m365_copilot_user_inventory.csv; save security_uc_13_4_8_m365_copilot_exfil_risk every fifteen minutes with earliest=-24h@h; route CRITICAL and HIGH to insider-threat queues; integrate ServiceNow REST ticketing; archive weekly evidence with actor and ObjectId facets redacted per policy.

## Evidence

Saved search security_uc_13_4_8_m365_copilot_exfil_risk on a fifteen-minute schedule; ESCU Microsoft Cloud analytic story drilldowns; weekly CSV exports of actor and ObjectId facets archived to a restricted insider_evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a tenant test slice, generate CopilotInteraction and FileDownload audit rows tied to Highly Confidential labels for a non-production principal without an active review_window in m365_copilot_user_inventory.csv, add a matching MCAS high score or ESCU notable on Alerts, and confirm security_uc_13_4_8_m365_copilot_exfil_risk returns CRITICAL or HIGH with non-null recommended_action within two fifteen-minute windows.

### Negative scenario

After removing test traffic, refresh the inventory CSV with an active review_window for the principal and confirm severities fall to LOW and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to CRITICAL and HIGH only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with Microsoft 365 tenant administrators, information protection owners who govern Microsoft Purview sensitivity labels and retention, identity engineers operating Microsoft Entra ID Conditional Access, Defender for Cloud Apps connector owners, Splunk Enterprise Security content engineers who maintain ESCU Microsoft Cloud analytic story enablement, and insider-threat program leads who coordinate with human resources and legal on Copilot telemetry reviews. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.4.1 managed-provider latency analytics, not UC-13.4.2 token cost and FinOps pacing, not UC-13.4.3 accelerator duty-cycle economics, not UC-13.4.4 model artifact versioning drift, not UC-13.4.5 AI gateway throttle fairness, not UC-13.4.6 self-hosted Ollama endpoint abuse, not UC-13.4.7 Model Context Protocol suspicious tool invocation, not UC-13.4.9 prompt injection content manipulation, not UC-13.4.10 cryptographic key rotation governance, not UC-13.4.11 content policy violation classifiers, not UC-13.4.13 sequence-to-sequence anomaly baselines, and not UC-13.4.15 Splunk MLTK drift statistics. The narrative here is Microsoft 365 Copilot grounding on tenant content in SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business, Microsoft Teams, Exchange Online, and Loop, where legitimate read access can become an indirect data spill when summarization and generation surface Highly Confidential or Restricted material to a broad internal audience faster than traditional browsing patterns. The analytic aligns Copilot audit telemetry with insider-threat ESCU stories, Purview Data Loss Prevention signals, and Defender for Cloud Apps risky activity scoring rather than adversarial prompt strings or model-host abuse.

Activate Microsoft 365 unified audit logging for Copilot workloads and confirm Workload equals Copilot appears in the administrative audit catalog. Maintain a Purview sensitivity label catalog that names Highly Confidential and Restricted tiers, maps protection templates, and documents eyes-on review expectations for eDiscovery officers who may legitimately use Copilot to summarize hold sets under supervision. Deploy the Defender for Cloud Apps connector with API permissions that cover OAuth app governance and risky OAuth consent where your subscription supports those signals. Install Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 and Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services with HTTP Event Collector or modular inputs aligned to your reference architecture. Enable Splunk Enterprise Security ESCU content for the Microsoft Cloud analytic story family so notables and risk scores can fuse with raw audit lanes when correlation searches are licensed.

RBAC must separate SOC analysts, human resources investigators, and legal eDiscovery teams who need different field visibility on CopilotInteraction payloads. Copilot prompts and model responses may contain personally identifiable information; restrict indexes and search filters accordingly. Maintain an allow-listed review_window column inside m365_copilot_user_inventory.csv for principals performing court-ordered discovery so detections downgrade inside documented calendar windows rather than muting the control globally.

Map detections to MITRE ATT&CK techniques T1530 Data from Cloud Storage Object, T1213.002 SharePoint, and T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service to anchor executive briefings and purple-team traceability. Document GDPR and HIPAA considerations where Copilot audit retention intersects data subject access requests and minimum necessary review for workforce monitoring. Assign CSV ownership for m365_copilot_user_inventory.csv to identity governance with quarterly refresh from human resources systems and license reconciliation jobs.

Risk briefing: CopilotInteraction events describe user prompts and model-assisted outcomes tied to grounded documents; Search operations reflect SharePoint or organizational retrieval fan-out; FileDownload and FileSyncDownloadedFull reflect concrete egress paths that pair with sensitivity labels for exfiltration hypotheses. Service Health incidents can duplicate or delay audit delivery; require _indextime guards before executive escalation.

Differentiation recap: insider exfiltration via Copilot grounding and DLP correlation, not LLM API latency dashboards, not token spend analytics, not GPU thermals, not model registry drift, not gateway rate fairness, not self-hosted inference abuse, not MCP tool sprawl, not prompt injection strings, not key rotation ceremonies, not content policy classifiers alone, not seq2seq baselines, not classical ML drift.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes that multisearch fans wide: Copilot unified audit JSON with Workload equals Copilot, Purview Information Protection and DLP style events on sourcetype o365 purview dlp, Defender for Cloud Apps activities on sourcetype o365 mcas, Enterprise Security Alerts summaries via tstats on the Alerts data model, and Entra ID sign-in telemetry on azure eventhub signin sourcetype for step-up and risk context prior to Copilot sessions.

Lane A, Copilot audit: forward Microsoft 365 Management Activity API exports through the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 so Operation values such as CopilotInteraction, Search, FileDownload, and FileSyncDownloadedFull retain UserId, ObjectId, Workload, and SensitivityLabel when present. Preserve ExtendedProperties JSON for advanced grounding metadata without logging raw prompt text in restricted regions when legal counsel requires redaction.

Lane B, Purview DLP: land events that carry policy matches, sensitive information types, and labeled file movement indicators. Normalize file paths and SharePoint site identifiers into object_id for correlation with Copilot ObjectId when hashes or stable IDs align.

Lane C, MCAS: ingest risky OAuth, mass download, impossible travel, or anomaly scores. Map RiskScore into mcas_score for severity fusion and ensure actor keys align with Entra user principal names.

Lane D, Alerts tstats: accelerate the Splunk CIM Alerts data model on Enterprise Security notables tied to Microsoft Cloud ESCU content. Use summariesonly true during steady state and widen time windows during investigations when summaries lag.

Lane E, Entra sign-in: stream sign-ins with risk detail fields into index azure sourcetype azure eventhub signin. Align clocks across Microsoft Graph export and Splunk indexers to avoid streamstats distortion.

Lookup governance: publish m365_copilot_user_inventory.csv with columns user_id, upn, department, manager, classification, license_sku, is_executive, review_window, and baseline_daily_events. Refresh after every major license change, acquisition integration, or contractor onboarding wave. Store the CSV in the search app or a dedicated security context app with version control.

props.conf guidance: TRUNCATE safely for wide Management Activity payloads, honor TIMESTAMP_FIELDS from Microsoft export times, and FIELDALIAS ladders that absorb renamed Graph fields across add-on upgrades.

Pre-save validation: confirm each lane returns rows in the last hour during business traffic, that a synthetic CopilotInteraction in staging appears with Workload Copilot, and that join to the inventory lookup resolves department for a test principal.

Dashboards: host a Copilot governance dashboard with severity-colored actor rows, drilldowns to raw audit JSON, MCAS policy context, DLP policy names, Entra risk attributes, and ESCU notable links.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as security_uc_13_4_8_m365_copilot_exfil_risk with a fifteen-minute schedule during production, dispatch earliest=-24h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is CRITICAL or HIGH after deduplication keyed on actor and peak_burst. Throttle duplicate actor rows for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, lanes, and labels in pager bodies so responders open Purview, MCAS, and Entra consoles without guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch merges Copilot audit, Purview DLP, MCAS, Alerts tstats, and Entra sign-in so a silent single lane cannot hide cross-plane insider exfil when Copilot accelerates access to labeled content. coalesce() ladders absorb UserId, UPN, user, and UserPrincipalName drift across sourcetypes. join to m365_copilot_user_inventory.csv supplies is_executive, review_window, and baseline_daily_events for case-driven severity. streamstats time_window equals two hours measures event bursts per actor. eventstats adds org-wide percentile baselines for burst counts. case() maps executive plus high risk plus Highly Confidential plus exfil operations into CRITICAL, bulk bursts without approved review windows into HIGH, single-file anomalies into MEDIUM, and approved review windows into LOW.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.8 Microsoft 365 Copilot insider exfil correlation: Copilot unified audit, Purview DLP, Defender for Cloud Apps, Enterprise Security Alerts tstats, Entra ID sign-in. Tunables earliest=-24h@h burst_ratio=2.0")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=o365 sourcetype=o365:audit Workload=Copilot earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="copilot_audit"
      | eval operation=trim(toString(coalesce(Operation,operation,"")))
      | eval object_id=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(ObjectId,ObjectID,object_id,""))),1,512)
      | eval sensitivity_label=trim(toString(coalesce(SensitivityLabel,sensitivity_label,Label,InformationProtectionLabelId,"")))
      | eval actor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(UserId,UPN,user,UserPrincipalName,UserKey,""))))
      | eval exfil_op=if(match(operation,"(?i)FileDownload|FileSyncDownloadedFull"),1,if(match(operation,"(?i)CopilotInteraction") AND match(sensitivity_label,"(?i)Highly Confidential|Restricted|Top Secret"),1,if(match(operation,"(?i)Search") AND match(sensitivity_label,"(?i)Highly Confidential"),1,0)))
      | eval risk_level="medium"
      | eval mcas_score=0
      | fields _time lane actor operation object_id sensitivity_label exfil_op risk_level mcas_score ]
    [ search index=o365 sourcetype=o365:purview:dlp earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="purview_dlp"
      | eval operation=trim(toString(coalesce(Operation,PolicyName,Workload,event_type,"purview_dlp")))
      | eval object_id=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(ObjectId,FileName,TargetPath,SiteUrl,""))),1,512)
      | eval sensitivity_label=trim(toString(coalesce(SensitivityLabel,Label,SensitiveInformationType,"")))
      | eval actor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(UserId,UPN,user,UserPrincipalName,""))))
      | eval exfil_op=if(match(operation,"(?i)share|upload|copy|transfer|unallowed"),1,0)
      | eval risk_level="high"
      | eval mcas_score=0
      | fields _time lane actor operation object_id sensitivity_label exfil_op risk_level mcas_score ]
    [ search index=o365 sourcetype=o365:mcas earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="mcas"
      | eval operation=trim(toString(coalesce(ActivityType,Action,Operation,activity_type,"mcas")))
      | eval object_id=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(ObjectName,FileName,URL,object_id,""))),1,512)
      | eval sensitivity_label=trim(toString(coalesce(SensitivityLabel,PolicyMatch,"")))
      | eval mcas_score=tonumber(coalesce(RiskScore,Score,risk_score,0))
      | eval actor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(UserId,UPN,user,UserPrincipalName,UserEmail,""))))
      | eval exfil_op=if(mcas_score>=70 OR match(operation,"(?i)mass|bulk|impossible|anomaly"),1,0)
      | eval risk_level=if(mcas_score>=80,"high",if(mcas_score>=50,"medium","low"))
      | fields _time lane actor operation object_id sensitivity_label exfil_op risk_level mcas_score ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t count AS alert_cnt latest(_time) AS _time FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.user
      | rename Alerts.user AS actor
      | eval lane="es_alerts"
      | eval actor=lower(trim(toString(actor)))
      | eval operation="escu_notable_fusion"
      | eval object_id="notable_correlation"
      | eval sensitivity_label=""
      | eval exfil_op=if(alert_cnt>=1,1,0)
      | eval risk_level="high"
      | eval mcas_score=0
      | fields _time lane actor operation object_id sensitivity_label exfil_op risk_level mcas_score ]
    [ search index=azure sourcetype=azure:eventhub:signin earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="entra_signin"
      | eval operation=trim(toString(coalesce(Operation,displayName,ActivityDisplayName,activityDisplayName,"signin")))
      | eval object_id=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(AppDisplayName,ResourceDisplayName,appDisplayName,""))),1,256)
      | eval sensitivity_label=""
      | eval actor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(userId,userPrincipalName,UserId,UPN,user,""))))
      | eval exfil_op=0
      | eval risk_level=if(match(RiskDetail,"(?i)high|hidden|anonymous") OR match(RiskLevelAggregated,"high") OR match(riskLevel,"high"),"high","low")
      | eval mcas_score=0
      | fields _time lane actor operation object_id sensitivity_label exfil_op risk_level mcas_score ]
| eval actor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(actor,""))))
| where len(actor)>3
| eval mcas_score=tonumber(coalesce(mcas_score,0))
| eval exfil_op=tonumber(coalesce(exfil_op,0))
| sort 0 actor + _time
| streamstats time_window=2h count AS burst_2h dc(operation) AS ops_dc BY actor
| eventstats perc75(burst_2h) AS org_p75_burst perc90(burst_2h) AS org_p90_burst
| join type=left max=0 actor
    [| inputlookup m365_copilot_user_inventory.csv
     | eval actor=lower(trim(toString(upn)))
     | fields actor user_id upn department manager classification license_sku is_executive review_window baseline_daily_events ]
| eval is_exec_l=lower(trim(toString(is_executive)))
| eval in_review=if(isnotnull(review_window) AND len(trim(toString(review_window)))>0 AND match(trim(toString(review_window)),"(?i)yes|true|active|202"),1,0)
| eval baseline_daily=tonumber(coalesce(baseline_daily_events,25))
| eval sensitivity_label=trim(toString(coalesce(sensitivity_label,"")))
| eval risk_lower=lower(trim(toString(risk_level)))
| eval severity=case(
    match(is_exec_l,"(?i)true|yes|1") AND risk_lower="high" AND match(sensitivity_label,"(?i)highly confidential|restricted") AND exfil_op>=1 AND in_review==0, "CRITICAL",
    burst_2h>=coalesce(org_p90_burst,999)*2 AND in_review==0 AND exfil_op>=1, "HIGH",
    burst_2h>baseline_daily*3 AND match(sensitivity_label,"(?i)confidential|secret|restricted"), "HIGH",
    burst_2h>8 AND match(sensitivity_label,"(?i)highly confidential"), "MEDIUM",
    in_review==1 OR burst_2h<=baseline_daily, "LOW",
    true(), "LOW")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="CRITICAL","open_insider_case_step_up_mfa_purview_hold_escalate_hr_legal",
    severity=="HIGH","correlate_mcas_dlp_review_copilot_sites_conditional_access_tighten",
    severity=="MEDIUM","analyst_triage_review_sharepoint_path_eyes_on_summary",
    true(),"refresh_inventory_lookup_tune_baselines")
| stats max(_time) AS last_time values(lane) AS lanes max(burst_2h) AS peak_burst max(org_p75_burst) AS org_p75 max(org_p90_burst) AS org_p90 values(sensitivity_label) AS labels values(risk_level) AS risks max(exfil_op) AS exfil_peak max(mcas_score) AS mcas_peak max(ops_dc) AS op_dc values(operation) AS operations first(severity) AS severity first(recommended_action) AS recommended_action values(department) AS department first(classification) AS classification first(license_sku) AS license_sku first(manager) AS manager first(review_window) AS review_window first(baseline_daily_events) AS baseline_daily_events BY actor user_id upn
| table last_time actor user_id upn lanes peak_burst org_p75 org_p90 labels risks exfil_peak mcas_peak op_dc severity recommended_action department
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[security_uc_13_4_8_m365_copilot_exfil_risk_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = insider-threat-platform@example.com
action.email.subject = M365 Copilot exfil risk $result.severity$ actor=$result.actor$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch security_uc_13_4_8_m365_copilot_exfil_risk | where severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above objectives, materialize per-lane summaries hourly into summary indexes keyed on actor, widen alert windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Purview audit search and Microsoft 365 compliance portals: compare CopilotInteraction counts per actor in the last four hours against Splunk aggregates for the same window. When totals disagree, verify Management Activity subscription health, connector credentials, and duplicate forwarder submissions before muting detections.

Controlled red-team validation: provision a non-production principal with eDiscovery rights and intentional access to an Information Barrier isolated site collection; perform bulk Copilot summarization across labeled hold material under legal supervision; confirm the detection surfaces CRITICAL or HIGH with exfil_peak and labels matching Highly Confidential when review_window is inactive. Repeat without Information Barrier misconfiguration to confirm benign baselines return LOW when review_window is active for the same principal.

Deep dive with exemplar ObjectId values: trace them through SharePoint unified audit, DLP incidents, and MCAS file activities. Confirm streamstats burst_2h aligns with interactive session length rather than indexer batch replay.

Cross-check Entra risk_level with Conditional Access sign-in logs for the same actor minutes before Copilot peaks. Negative test: after removing test traffic and refreshing inventory CSV, confirm filtering severity to CRITICAL and HIGH returns zero rows for six consecutive runs in a quiet tenant slice.

Runbook linkage: document approved legal hold review programs so validators do not file defects during supervised eDiscovery that intentionally elevates Copilot usage.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Operationalize the detection with a joint SOC and human resources escalation path for CRITICAL rows, including documented handoffs when workforce policy may apply. Trigger automatic step-up multifactor authentication through Microsoft Entra Conditional Access policies mapped to Splunk alert tags so risky Copilot sessions meet stronger proof requirements without waiting for manual ticket routing. Integrate Enterprise Security ESCU notables into episode management with clear ownership queues and linkbacks to this saved search. Run a weekly insider-threat review that samples HIGH severity outcomes, confirms inventory CSV freshness, and challenges stale baselines. Automate ServiceNow incident creation through Splunk alert actions using the REST API with least-privilege integration credentials and redacted narrative fields. Publish a Copilot governance dashboard that shows rolling severity counts, top actors, MCAS score distributions, and Entra risk overlays for leadership transparency.

Case A — Unified audit ingest lag exceeds twenty-four hours so CopilotInteraction rows arrive after the insider already exported material; correlate Microsoft 365 Service Health and Splunk _indextime skew before lowering severity.

Case B — SensitivityLabel is blank because Purview Information Protection licensing or automatic labeling is not fully deployed; tune the HIGH tier to require alternate fields such as site classification or DLP policy name before paging executives.

Case C — Microsoft Graph throttles the Defender for Cloud Apps connector during peak hours; MCAS lanes drop while Copilot audit continues, producing one-sided narratives until backoff windows clear.

Case D — Copilot Pages and Loop auto-summarization fans duplicate Search operations that double burst_2h counts for power users; dedupe on ObjectId hashes when stable identifiers exist.

Case E — user_id case sensitivity drifts after tenant rename or SMTP proxy changes break join keys; normalize actor with lower() on both sides and reconcile UPN formats weekly.

Case F — Information Barriers fail to constrain Copilot grounding in a pilot ring; legal holds still show benign labels while Copilot surfaces barred content—treat as governance defect not SOC mute.

Case G — Conditional Access policies exempt legacy clients so high risk_level sign-ins still reach Copilot; pair Entra lane with device compliance state before trusting LOW severity.

Case H — License SKU changes re-enable Copilot for dormant contractors, spiking burst_2h without malicious intent; refresh m365_copilot_user_inventory.csv after every Enterprise Agreement change window.

Case I — tstats against Alerts returns zero rows because notable index acceleration broke or datamodel summaries were disabled; repair ES maintenance jobs before declaring the tenant quiet.

Case J — review_window CSV rows stay active after eDiscovery matter closes, suppressing true positives; automate expiry when holds lift and audit the CSV quarterly.

Case K — MITRE technique labels on ESCU stories drift from ATT&CK releases; re-map T1530, T1213.002, and T1567 references during quarterly purple-team reviews.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.8 Microsoft 365 Copilot insider exfil correlation: Copilot unified audit, Purview DLP, Defender for Cloud Apps, Enterprise Security Alerts tstats, Entra ID sign-in. Tunables earliest=-24h@h burst_ratio=2.0")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=o365 sourcetype=o365:audit Workload=Copilot earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="copilot_audit"
      | eval operation=trim(toString(coalesce(Operation,operation,"")))
      | eval object_id=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(ObjectId,ObjectID,object_id,""))),1,512)
      | eval sensitivity_label=trim(toString(coalesce(SensitivityLabel,sensitivity_label,Label,InformationProtectionLabelId,"")))
      | eval actor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(UserId,UPN,user,UserPrincipalName,UserKey,""))))
      | eval exfil_op=if(match(operation,"(?i)FileDownload|FileSyncDownloadedFull"),1,if(match(operation,"(?i)CopilotInteraction") AND match(sensitivity_label,"(?i)Highly Confidential|Restricted|Top Secret"),1,if(match(operation,"(?i)Search") AND match(sensitivity_label,"(?i)Highly Confidential"),1,0)))
      | eval risk_level="medium"
      | eval mcas_score=0
      | fields _time lane actor operation object_id sensitivity_label exfil_op risk_level mcas_score ]
    [ search index=o365 sourcetype=o365:purview:dlp earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="purview_dlp"
      | eval operation=trim(toString(coalesce(Operation,PolicyName,Workload,event_type,"purview_dlp")))
      | eval object_id=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(ObjectId,FileName,TargetPath,SiteUrl,""))),1,512)
      | eval sensitivity_label=trim(toString(coalesce(SensitivityLabel,Label,SensitiveInformationType,"")))
      | eval actor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(UserId,UPN,user,UserPrincipalName,""))))
      | eval exfil_op=if(match(operation,"(?i)share|upload|copy|transfer|unallowed"),1,0)
      | eval risk_level="high"
      | eval mcas_score=0
      | fields _time lane actor operation object_id sensitivity_label exfil_op risk_level mcas_score ]
    [ search index=o365 sourcetype=o365:mcas earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="mcas"
      | eval operation=trim(toString(coalesce(ActivityType,Action,Operation,activity_type,"mcas")))
      | eval object_id=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(ObjectName,FileName,URL,object_id,""))),1,512)
      | eval sensitivity_label=trim(toString(coalesce(SensitivityLabel,PolicyMatch,"")))
      | eval mcas_score=tonumber(coalesce(RiskScore,Score,risk_score,0))
      | eval actor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(UserId,UPN,user,UserPrincipalName,UserEmail,""))))
      | eval exfil_op=if(mcas_score>=70 OR match(operation,"(?i)mass|bulk|impossible|anomaly"),1,0)
      | eval risk_level=if(mcas_score>=80,"high",if(mcas_score>=50,"medium","low"))
      | fields _time lane actor operation object_id sensitivity_label exfil_op risk_level mcas_score ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t count AS alert_cnt latest(_time) AS _time FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.user
      | rename Alerts.user AS actor
      | eval lane="es_alerts"
      | eval actor=lower(trim(toString(actor)))
      | eval operation="escu_notable_fusion"
      | eval object_id="notable_correlation"
      | eval sensitivity_label=""
      | eval exfil_op=if(alert_cnt>=1,1,0)
      | eval risk_level="high"
      | eval mcas_score=0
      | fields _time lane actor operation object_id sensitivity_label exfil_op risk_level mcas_score ]
    [ search index=azure sourcetype=azure:eventhub:signin earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="entra_signin"
      | eval operation=trim(toString(coalesce(Operation,displayName,ActivityDisplayName,activityDisplayName,"signin")))
      | eval object_id=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(AppDisplayName,ResourceDisplayName,appDisplayName,""))),1,256)
      | eval sensitivity_label=""
      | eval actor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(userId,userPrincipalName,UserId,UPN,user,""))))
      | eval exfil_op=0
      | eval risk_level=if(match(RiskDetail,"(?i)high|hidden|anonymous") OR match(RiskLevelAggregated,"high") OR match(riskLevel,"high"),"high","low")
      | eval mcas_score=0
      | fields _time lane actor operation object_id sensitivity_label exfil_op risk_level mcas_score ]
| eval actor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(actor,""))))
| where len(actor)>3
| eval mcas_score=tonumber(coalesce(mcas_score,0))
| eval exfil_op=tonumber(coalesce(exfil_op,0))
| sort 0 actor + _time
| streamstats time_window=2h count AS burst_2h dc(operation) AS ops_dc BY actor
| eventstats perc75(burst_2h) AS org_p75_burst perc90(burst_2h) AS org_p90_burst
| join type=left max=0 actor
    [| inputlookup m365_copilot_user_inventory.csv
     | eval actor=lower(trim(toString(upn)))
     | fields actor user_id upn department manager classification license_sku is_executive review_window baseline_daily_events ]
| eval is_exec_l=lower(trim(toString(is_executive)))
| eval in_review=if(isnotnull(review_window) AND len(trim(toString(review_window)))>0 AND match(trim(toString(review_window)),"(?i)yes|true|active|202"),1,0)
| eval baseline_daily=tonumber(coalesce(baseline_daily_events,25))
| eval sensitivity_label=trim(toString(coalesce(sensitivity_label,"")))
| eval risk_lower=lower(trim(toString(risk_level)))
| eval severity=case(
    match(is_exec_l,"(?i)true|yes|1") AND risk_lower="high" AND match(sensitivity_label,"(?i)highly confidential|restricted") AND exfil_op>=1 AND in_review==0, "CRITICAL",
    burst_2h>=coalesce(org_p90_burst,999)*2 AND in_review==0 AND exfil_op>=1, "HIGH",
    burst_2h>baseline_daily*3 AND match(sensitivity_label,"(?i)confidential|secret|restricted"), "HIGH",
    burst_2h>8 AND match(sensitivity_label,"(?i)highly confidential"), "MEDIUM",
    in_review==1 OR burst_2h<=baseline_daily, "LOW",
    true(), "LOW")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="CRITICAL","open_insider_case_step_up_mfa_purview_hold_escalate_hr_legal",
    severity=="HIGH","correlate_mcas_dlp_review_copilot_sites_conditional_access_tighten",
    severity=="MEDIUM","analyst_triage_review_sharepoint_path_eyes_on_summary",
    true(),"refresh_inventory_lookup_tune_baselines")
| stats max(_time) AS last_time values(lane) AS lanes max(burst_2h) AS peak_burst max(org_p75_burst) AS org_p75 max(org_p90_burst) AS org_p90 values(sensitivity_label) AS labels values(risk_level) AS risks max(exfil_op) AS exfil_peak max(mcas_score) AS mcas_peak max(ops_dc) AS op_dc values(operation) AS operations first(severity) AS severity first(recommended_action) AS recommended_action values(department) AS department first(classification) AS classification first(license_sku) AS license_sku first(manager) AS manager first(review_window) AS review_window first(baseline_daily_events) AS baseline_daily_events BY actor user_id upn
| table last_time actor user_id upn lanes peak_burst org_p75 org_p90 labels risks exfil_peak mcas_peak op_dc severity recommended_action department
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Alerts.severity) AS sev count FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.user
| rename Alerts.user AS actor
| table actor sev count
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by actor with drilldowns to o365 audit Copilot operations, Purview DLP incidents, MCAS risky activities, Entra sign-in risk, ESCU notable links, and inventory attributes.

## Known False Positives

Legitimate legal and finance research workflows often summarize many sensitivity-labeled contracts in short windows; require review_window allow lists and department-based suppressions before paging line managers. Copilot agent flows and Microsoft Search can produce surges of Search operations that resemble scraping; pair ObjectId diversity with MCAS mass-download heuristics rather than volume alone. eDiscovery officers with eyes-on review may intentionally drive high CopilotInteraction counts across hold sets; document matter IDs in the inventory CSV. Microsoft 365 Service Health incidents can duplicate or delay audit events, creating bursty backfills that look like exfiltration until _indextime latency is checked. License type changes can suddenly enable Copilot for populations that were previously ineligible, elevating event volume without malice. Mergers and acquisitions due diligence rooms may authorize bulk access to Highly Confidential libraries; align classification column in the inventory CSV with transaction calendars. Purview label drift after content migrations can mis-tag files until rescans finish, causing false HIGH tiers until labels stabilize. Copilot Pages and Loop auto-summarization features can inflate per-user counts during legitimate collaboration sprints; tune baseline_daily_events upward for product and marketing cohorts after enablement waves.
